== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7200005005298 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県大垣市上石津町宮字街道194-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:大神神社 (大垣市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11438336}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Ōmiwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:大垣市上石津町宮 大神神社.jpg|270px]] | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōgaki|Q384926|WD=Q384926|1=Ōgaki}} Kamiishizu-chō Miya, Kaidō 194-1 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} | 創建={{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kamiishizu-chō, {{ill|lt=Ōgaki|Q384926|WD=Q384926|1=Ōgaki}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of Tagi District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is one of the many {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrines|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} in Japan. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} Associated deities *{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarehiko-no-Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}} *{{ill|lt=Himetataraisuzuhime|Q5353793|WD=Q5353793|lt=Himetataraisuzuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Himetataraisuzu-hime}} === History === *The founding date is unknown. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Jitō|Q232026|WD=Q232026|1=Empress Jitō}}, when she reached Yokkaichi District in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}} on an imperial progress to Ise, Right Minister Miwa no Ason Takachimaro received an oracle from Ōmononushi Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto and founded this shrine on Mt. Tagi in Mino Province. It was originally called Ōmiwa Shrine. *In {{ill|lt=1113|Q19531|WD=Q19531|1=1113}} (Eikyū 1), a nearby shrine was washed to this shrine in a violent storm. The deity of that shrine (Emperor Jinmu and Himetataraisuzuhime-no-Mikoto) was merged here, and the name was changed to Nagarehiko Daimyōjin. *In {{ill|lt=1375|Q6277|WD=Q6277|1=1375}} (Eiwa 7), it was moved to the current site. *In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), it resumed the name Ōmiwa Shrine; in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it became gōsha. === Cultural properties === '''Gifu Prefecture designated natural monument''' *Shrine grove of Ōmiwa Shrine{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/oogakisi/oomiwa.html |title=Shrine grove of Ōmiwa Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-25}} === Access === *From {{ill|lt=Sekigahara Station|Q872034|WD=Q872034|1=Sekigahara Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}}, take the {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu Bus|Q11415818|WD=Q11415818|1=Meihan Kintetsu Bus}} Sekigahara-Toki line and get off at “Miya.” === Nearby facilities === *{{ill|lt=Nishitakagi Family Jinya|Q11630008|WD=Q11630008|1=Nishitakagike Jin'ya}} === Other === *Shrine tradition gives the founding year as Shuchō 6, but that year does not exist. Since Shuchō 1 is {{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619|1=686}}, “Shuchō 6” is estimated around {{ill|lt=691|Q23474|WD=Q23474|1=691}}, suggesting a late 7th-century foundation. *The current site is in former Ishi District, Mino Province. It moved here in Eiwa 7; before that it is said to have been at a place called Uenoyama. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oomiwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oomiwa Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ogaki]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Ogaki]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7200005005298 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県大垣市上石津町宮字街道194-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:大神神社 (大垣市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11438336}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Ōmiwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:大垣市上石津町宮 大神神社.jpg|270px]] | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōgaki|Q384926|WD=Q384926|1=Ōgaki}} Kamiishizu-chō Miya, Kaidō 194-1 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} | 創建={{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kamiishizu-chō, {{ill|lt=Ōgaki|Q384926|WD=Q384926|1=Ōgaki}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of Tagi District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is one of the many {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrines|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} in Japan. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} Associated deities *{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarehiko-no-Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}} *{{ill|lt=Himetataraisuzuhime|Q5353793|WD=Q5353793|lt=Himetataraisuzuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Himetataraisuzu-hime}} === History === *The founding date is unknown. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Jitō|Q232026|WD=Q232026|1=Empress Jitō}}, when she reached Yokkaichi District in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}} on an imperial progress to Ise, Right Minister Miwa no Ason Takachimaro received an oracle from Ōmononushi Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto and founded this shrine on Mt. Tagi in Mino Province. It was originally called Ōmiwa Shrine. *In {{ill|lt=1113|Q19531|WD=Q19531|1=1113}} (Eikyū 1), a nearby shrine was washed to this shrine in a violent storm. The deity of that shrine (Emperor Jinmu and Himetataraisuzuhime-no-Mikoto) was merged here, and the name was changed to Nagarehiko Daimyōjin. *In {{ill|lt=1375|Q6277|WD=Q6277|1=1375}} (Eiwa 7), it was moved to the current site. *In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), it resumed the name Ōmiwa Shrine; in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it became gōsha. === Cultural properties === '''Gifu Prefecture designated natural monument''' *Shrine grove of Ōmiwa Shrine{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/oogakisi/oomiwa.html |title=Shrine grove of Ōmiwa Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-25}} === Access === *From {{ill|lt=Sekigahara Station|Q872034|WD=Q872034|1=Sekigahara Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}}, take the {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu Bus|Q11415818|WD=Q11415818|1=Meihan Kintetsu Bus}} Sekigahara-Toki line and get off at “Miya.” === Nearby facilities === *{{ill|lt=Nishitakagi Family Jinya|Q11630008|WD=Q11630008|1=Nishitakagike Jin'ya}} === Other === *Shrine tradition gives the founding year as Shuchō 6, but that year does not exist. Since Shuchō 1 is {{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619|1=686}}, “Shuchō 6” is estimated around {{ill|lt=691|Q23474|WD=Q23474|1=691}}, suggesting a late 7th-century foundation. *The current site is in former Ishi District, Mino Province. It moved here in Eiwa 7; before that it is said to have been at a place called Uenoyama. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oomiwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oomiwa Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ogaki]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ogaki]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7200005005298 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県大垣市上石津町宮字街道194-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:大神神社 (大垣市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11438336}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Ōmiwa Shrine | 画像=[[File:大垣市上石津町宮 大神神社.jpg|270px]] | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōgaki|Q384926|WD=Q384926|1=Ōgaki}} Kamiishizu-chō Miya, Kaidō 194-1 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} | 創建={{ill|lt=7th century|Q8089|WD=Q8089|1=7th century}} | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 例祭={{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kamiishizu-chō, {{ill|lt=Ōgaki|Q384926|WD=Q384926|1=Ōgaki}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of Tagi District, {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is one of the many {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrines|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} in Japan. === Enshrined deities === *{{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto|1=Ōmononushi}} Associated deities *{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Kamuyamatoiwarehiko-no-Sumeramikoto|1=Emperor Jimmu}} *{{ill|lt=Himetataraisuzuhime|Q5353793|WD=Q5353793|lt=Himetataraisuzuhime-no-Mikoto|1=Himetataraisuzu-hime}} === History === *The founding date is unknown. During the reign of {{ill|lt=Empress Jitō|Q232026|WD=Q232026|1=Empress Jitō}}, when she reached Yokkaichi District in {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|WD=Q864166|1=Ise Province}} on an imperial progress to Ise, Right Minister Miwa no Ason Takachimaro received an oracle from Ōmononushi Kushimikatama-no-Mikoto and founded this shrine on Mt. Tagi in Mino Province. It was originally called Ōmiwa Shrine. *In {{ill|lt=1113|Q19531|WD=Q19531|1=1113}} (Eikyū 1), a nearby shrine was washed to this shrine in a violent storm. The deity of that shrine (Emperor Jinmu and Himetataraisuzuhime-no-Mikoto) was merged here, and the name was changed to Nagarehiko Daimyōjin. *In {{ill|lt=1375|Q6277|WD=Q6277|1=1375}} (Eiwa 7), it was moved to the current site. *In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), it resumed the name Ōmiwa Shrine; in Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it became gōsha. === Cultural properties === '''Gifu Prefecture designated natural monument''' *Shrine grove of Ōmiwa Shrine{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/oogakisi/oomiwa.html |title=Shrine grove of Ōmiwa Shrine |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-25}} === Access === *From {{ill|lt=Sekigahara Station|Q872034|WD=Q872034|1=Sekigahara Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line|Q1190152|WD=Q1190152|1=Tōkaidō Main Line}}, take the {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu Bus|Q11415818|WD=Q11415818|1=Meihan Kintetsu Bus}} Sekigahara-Toki line and get off at “Miya.” === Nearby facilities === *{{ill|lt=Nishitakagi Family Jinya|Q11630008|WD=Q11630008|1=Nishitakagike Jin'ya}} === Other === *Shrine tradition gives the founding year as Shuchō 6, but that year does not exist. Since Shuchō 1 is {{ill|lt=686|Q30619|WD=Q30619|1=686}}, “Shuchō 6” is estimated around {{ill|lt=691|Q23474|WD=Q23474|1=691}}, suggesting a late 7th-century foundation. *The current site is in former Ishi District, Mino Province. It moved here in Eiwa 7; before that it is said to have been at a place called Uenoyama. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Oomiwa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Oomiwa Shrine]][[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ogaki]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Natural Monuments Designated by Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ogaki]]
